cases.reason: Indefinite suspension pending neurological clearance following his knockout loss to Robelis Despaigne
cases.source_quote: Junior dos Santos: Indefinite suspension pending neurological clearance; 45-day suspension with 30 days no contact

cases.reason: 180-day suspension unless cleared by physician for a possible fracture in the left-hand finger, suffered during his bloody TKO loss to Mike Perry
cases.source_quote: Nate Diaz: 180-day suspension unless cleared by physician for possible fracture in left-hand finger; 60-day suspension unless cleared for scalp and forehead lacerations; 45 days suspended with 30 days no contact

cases.reason: 45-day suspension with 30 days no contact for her knockout (16-second TKO) loss to Germaine de Randamie at UFC Fight Night 155
cases.source_quote: Aspen Ladd: suspended 45 days with 30 days no contact (no exception) for knockout.

cases.reason: 180-day suspension (180 days no contact) or until cleared by physician for a possible orbital fracture; CT scan required, following his TKO loss at UFC Fight Night 155
cases.source_quote: Mike Rodriguez: suspended seven days; suspended 45 days with 30 days no contact (no exception) for hard bout; and suspended 180 days with 180 days no contact, or until cleared by physician, for possible orbital fracture. Catscan required.

cases.reason: 45-day suspension (30 days no contact) for TKO loss plus a 60-day suspension for a left eye laceration at UFC Fight Night 155
cases.source_quote: Mirsad Bektic: suspended 45 days with 30 days no contact (no exception) for TKO; suspended 60 days with 60 days no contact, or until cleared by physician, for left eye laceration.

cases.jurisdiction: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)
cases.reason: Indefinite medical suspension after his welterweight loss to Punahele Soriano at UFC Houston; transported to the hospital for precautionary CT scans and a leg scan. Needs medical clearance before returning.
cases.source_quote: two fighters from the “Prelims” undercard — Ramiz Brahimaj and Alden Coria — were hit with indefinite suspensions and will need medical clearance before they can return to action.

cases.jurisdiction: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)
cases.reason: Indefinite medical suspension following UFC Houston (won a unanimous decision over Luis Gurule); requires medical clearance before returning to action per the TDLR post-event suspension list.
cases.source_quote: Alden Coria: Suspended indefinitely

cases.jurisdiction: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)
cases.reason: 60-day medical suspension following his knockout loss to Uros Medic at UFC Houston (Toyota Center). TDLR handed out 28 medical suspensions after the event.
cases.source_quote: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR) handed out 28 medical suspensions following UFC Houston ... Geoff Neal: Suspended for 60 days

cases.reason: 60-day medical suspension with 45 days no contact after UFC 229 (Las Vegas); additionally must have a suspected nasal fracture cleared by a physician before returning.
cases.source_quote: Jalin Turner: Suspended 60 days with 45 days no contact. Additionally, must have nasal fracture cleared.

cases.reason: Conditional NSAC medical suspension after UFC 229: must have a nasal fracture cleared by a physician or otherwise serve a 180-day suspension.
cases.source_quote: Anthony Pettis: Must have nasal fracture cleared or face 180-day suspension

cases.reason: Medical/safety suspension after a one-sided loss in Hamburg due to the severity of punches taken; he fought in Argentina on July 20 (11 days before clearance) against Eduardo Abreu and died on July 25, 2019.
cases.source_quote: Santillan was told by the BDB that he must not fight until July 31 due to the severity of the punches he took.

cases.reason: Positive tests for the banned PED ostarine following his April 20, 2024 win over Devin Haney; the result was changed to a no-contest and he was fined.
cases.source_quote: Boxing star Ryan Garcia has been suspended one year after he reached a settlement with the New York State Athletic Commission following his positive tests for the performance-enhancing drug ostarine, the commission told ESPN on Thursday. The suspension is retroactive to Garcia's April 20 decision victory over Devin Haney, which has been overturned to a no-contest.

cases.reason: 30-day medical suspension cited as 'laceration/head blows' after the July 19, 2019 fight in which his opponent Maxim Dadashev suffered a fatal subdural hematoma and died on July 23, 2019.
cases.source_quote: He received a 30-day medical suspension from the Maryland State Athletic Commission, which cited 'laceration/head blows.'

cases.reason: Suspended nine months and fined (originally $900,000) for failing a drug test (positive for marijuana) following his Sept 15, 2012 loss to Sergio Martinez. He admitted smoking marijuana eight or nine days before the fight and had falsified his prefight medical questionnaire.
cases.source_quote: suspended nine months and fined $900,000 by the Nevada State Athletic Commission for failing a drug test following his loss to champion Sergio Martinez

cases.jurisdiction: New Jersey State Athletic Control Board (NJSACB)
cases.reason: Indefinite medical suspension pending orthopedist clearance of his left hand after UFC 328 (May 9, 2026, Newark, NJ), illustrating the standard commission-issued post-bout medical suspension that bars competing in any jurisdiction until cleared.
cases.source_quote: Sean Strickland: Indefinite suspension pending orthopedist clearance of left hand.

cases.jurisdiction: New Jersey State Athletic Control Board (NJSACB)
cases.reason: Indefinite suspension pending orthopedist clearance of her left shoulder and left elbow after losing the bantamweight title to Kayla Harrison by kimura at UFC 316 in Newark (June 7, 2025).
cases.source_quote: Julianna Peña: Indefinite suspension pending clearance from an orthopedist on her left shoulder and left elbow

cases.jurisdiction: New Jersey State Athletic Control Board (NJSACB)
cases.reason: 30-day no-contact suspension plus indefinite suspension pending x-rays and orthopedist clearance of his right foot after UFC 316 in Newark (June 7, 2025).
cases.source_quote: Serghei Spivac: 30 day suspension, no contact. Indefinite suspension pending x-rays of right foot and orthopedist clearance of right foot.

cases.jurisdiction: New Jersey State Athletic Control Board (NJSACB)
cases.reason: 30-day no-contact suspension plus indefinite suspension pending dentist clearance for a loose right maxillary incisor after UFC 316 in Newark (June 7, 2025).
cases.source_quote: Kelvin Gastelum: 30 day suspension, no contact. Indefinite suspension pending clearance from a dentist for loose right maxillary incisor

cases.reason: Indefinite medical suspension pending orthopedic clearance following his bantamweight title defense in the UFC 299 main event in Miami (March 9, 2024).
cases.source_quote: Sean O’Malley: Indefinite, pending orthopedic clearance

cases.reason: Indefinite medical suspension pending orthopedic clearance after his welterweight bout at UFC 299 in Miami (March 9, 2024).
cases.source_quote: Jack Della Maddalena: Indefinite, pending orthopedic clearance

cases.reason: Indefinite medical suspension pending neurological clearance after his welterweight bout at UFC 299 in Miami (March 9, 2024).
cases.source_quote: Gilbert Burns: Indefinite, pending neurological clearance

cases.reason: Knocked out by Chris Gutierrez in his retirement fight at UFC 281 at Madison Square Garden (Nov 12, 2022); suspended indefinitely with a mandatory minimum of 90 days, requiring doctor's clearance to return.
cases.source_quote: Among the 19 indefinite suspensions, five fighters – Frankie Edgar, Dominick Reyes, Nicolae Negumereanu, Seung Woo Choi, and Ottman Azaitar – received the longest mandatory minimum of 90 days.

cases.reason: Knocked out by Ryan Spann at UFC 281 at Madison Square Garden (Nov 12, 2022); suspended indefinitely with a mandatory minimum of 90 days, requiring doctor's clearance to return.
cases.source_quote: Among the 19 indefinite suspensions, five fighters – Frankie Edgar, Dominick Reyes, Nicolae Negumereanu, Seung Woo Choi, and Ottman Azaitar – received the longest mandatory minimum of 90 days.

cases.jurisdiction: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)
cases.reason: Knocked out in 64 seconds by Arman Tsarukyan in the UFC Austin main event (Dec 2, 2023); suspended a minimum of 60 days and 180 days unless medically cleared.
cases.source_quote: Suspended a minimum of 60 days and also suspended 180 days unless medically cleared.

cases.jurisdiction: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)
cases.reason: Stopped by Jalin Turner in a first-round finish at UFC Austin (Dec 2, 2023); suspended a minimum of 60 days and 180 days unless medically cleared.
cases.source_quote: Suspended a minimum of 60 days and also suspended 180 days unless medically cleared.

cases.reason: Suspended indefinitely after his win over Vitor Petrino at UFC on ESPN 63 in Tampa (Dec 14, 2024); per Florida state statute, injury specifics were not disclosed by the commission.
cases.source_quote: Dustin Jacoby: Suspended indefinitely.

cases.reason: Suspended indefinitely after his win over Billy Quarantillo at UFC on ESPN 63 in Tampa (Dec 14, 2024); per Florida state statute, injury specifics were not disclosed by the commission.
cases.source_quote: Cub Swanson: Suspended indefinitely.

cases.reason: Attempted to bite Roamer Alexis Angulo during their June 11, 2022 bout at Madison Square Garden; six-month suspension running from the fight date plus a $10,000 fine.
cases.source_quote: Edgar Berlanga is being suspended six months by the New York State Athletic Commission and fined $10,000 for attempting to bite Alexis Angulo on Saturday, sources told ESPN.

cases.reason: Positive out-of-competition drug test for GW501516 ahead of a scheduled July 9, 2020 bout against Jerry Forrest in Las Vegas; NSAC voted unanimously on Dec. 2, 2020 to ban him for two years, with a possible six-month reduction if he completed a PED treatment and VADA testing program.
cases.source_quote: The Nevada State Athletic Commission voted unanimously Wednesday to ban Miller for two years due to a positive drug test for the banned substance GW501516.

cases.reason: Failed drug test for the banned substance clomiphene, which pulled him from a scheduled May 13, 2023 bout against Rolando Romero in Las Vegas; the NSAC imposed a six-month suspension on June 20, 2023, with eligibility to return Oct. 5, 2023.
cases.source_quote: On Tuesday, the commission suspended Puello for six months.

cases.reason: Boxer's licence suspended on March 30, 2020 pending a misconduct hearing after he posted a social media video appearing to condone domestic violence during the COVID-19 lockdown.
cases.source_quote: The British Boxing Board of Control, having considered comments made by Billy Joe Saunders on social media, have suspended his boxer's license pending a hearing

cases.reason: Disqualified for biting David Price during their March 30, 2019 heavyweight bout in Liverpool; given a six-month ban and fined £10,000 at a hearing of the Board's central area council on April 13, 2019, with his fight purse withheld.
cases.source_quote: Kash Ali has been given a six-month ban for biting David Price during their all-British heavyweight fight.

cases.reason: Medical suspension after his 11th-round knockout loss to Tyson Fury on Oct. 9, 2021 in Las Vegas; taken to hospital after the fight and required surgery on a broken hand; not permitted to fight until April 8, 2022 unless cleared by a doctor.
cases.source_quote: A six-month suspension from the Nevada State Athletic Commission means Wilder will not be permitted to fight until April 8, unless his injury is cleared by a doctor.

cases.reason: Standard 45-day medical rest suspension after his gruelling 11th-round knockout win over Deontay Wilder in their Oct. 9, 2021 trilogy fight in Las Vegas, during which he was knocked down twice.
cases.source_quote: Tyson Fury has been handed a 45-day medical suspension after his knockout win against Deontay Wilder.

cases.reason: Third career marijuana offense; tested positive for marijuana metabolites after UFC 183 loss to Anderson Silva. Suspended five years and fined $165,000.
cases.source_quote: The Nevada State Athletic Commission suspended UFC welterweight Nick Diaz for five years on Monday because of the third marijuana-related offense of his career.

cases.reason: Post-fight brawl after UFC 229; suspended nine months retroactive to the Oct. 6, 2018 event date and fined $500,000.
cases.source_quote: The NSAC voted to suspend Nurmagomedov (27-0) for nine months and fined him $500,000 of a disclosed $2 million purse.

cases.reason: Post-fight brawl after UFC 229; NSAC suspension dated retroactively to the Oct. 6, 2018 event, eligible to return April 6, 2019, fined $50,000.
cases.source_quote: McGregor (21-4) received a six-month suspension and was fined $50,000.

cases.reason: Failed drug tests around UFC 183 (drostanolone, a form of androstane, and a banned anti-anxiety medication); one-year suspension retroactive to the Jan. 31, 2015 fight date and a $380,000 fine.
cases.source_quote: suspended one year by the Nevada State Athletic Commission because of multiple failed drug tests

cases.reason: Two positive tests for clenbuterol in random urine tests collected Feb. 17 and Feb. 20, 2018; six-month suspension backdated to the first positive test.
cases.source_quote: Alvarez's suspension will be lifted on Aug. 17, six months from Feb. 17, the date of his first positive drug test.

cases.reason: Tested positive for metabolites of the anabolic steroid turinabol at UFC 214; license revoked (effective Aug. 28, 2017, upheld Feb. 27, 2018) and fined $205,000, with no license until he reapplies and is approved.
cases.source_quote: The California State Athletic Commission voted unanimously on Tuesday to uphold the decision to revoke Jon Jones' license

cases.jurisdiction: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)
cases.reason: Post-knockout medical suspension after second-round KO loss to Tai Tuivasa at UFC 271 in Houston on Feb. 12, 2022; TDLR suspended him until April 14, 2022.
cases.source_quote: Due to the nature of the loss, Lewis will need to sit out from training until Apr. 14.

cases.jurisdiction: New Jersey State Athletic Control Board (NJSACB)
cases.reason: Medical suspension after UFC 302 submission loss to Islam Makhachev in Newark on June 1, 2024; indefinite pending ENT clearance for his nose and orthopedist clearance for his left knee.
cases.source_quote: Dustin Poirier: Indefinite suspension pending ENT clearance for nose, orthopedist clearance for left knee

cases.reason: License revoked and fined $3 million (10 percent of his $30 million purse) for biting Evander Holyfield's ears in the June 28, 1997 title bout; could not ask the commission to reconsider until at least July 1998.
cases.source_quote: the five-man Nevada State Athletic Commission unanimously voted to revoke Tyson's boxing license

cases.reason: Failed multiple drug tests (clomiphene) around his UFC 200 fight vs Mark Hunt; one-year suspension dated retroactively to July 9, 2016, fined $250,000 of his $2.5 million purse, win overturned to a no contest.
cases.source_quote: The Nevada State Athletic Commission has suspended Brock Lesnar one year for failing multiple drug tests around his mixed martial arts fight against Mark Hunt at UFC 200 on July 9.

cases.reason: Tested positive for recombinant EPO around his Jan. 19, 2019 flyweight title fight vs Henry Cejudo in Brooklyn; NYSAC issued its own one-year suspension, served concurrently with a two-year USADA ban dated retroactively to Jan. 18, 2019.
cases.source_quote: The New York State Athletic Commission issued Dillashaw its own one-year suspension for the failed drug test.

cases.reason: Missed weight by 2.3 kg for his WBC bantamweight title rematch vs Shinsuke Yamanaka (March 1, 2018); the JBC ethics committee imposed a permanent suspension banning him from boxing in Japan, announced March 9, 2018.
cases.source_quote: it will make the punishment a permanent suspension - thus banning Nery from boxing in Japan for life.

cases.reason: First-round knockout loss to Michael Morales at UFC 322 (Madison Square Garden, Nov 15, 2025); 90-day medical suspension requiring neurology clearance before return.
cases.source_quote: Sean Brady, who fell to Michael Morales via first-round knockout, faces a 90-day suspension with neurology clearance after his loss.

cases.reason: Knockout loss to Carlos Prates at UFC 322, the first KO loss of his career; 90-day medical suspension with mandatory neurology clearance.
cases.source_quote: Edwards was also handed a 90-day suspension with neurology clearance after he lost by knockout for the first time in his career

cases.jurisdiction: New Jersey State Athletic Control Board (NJSACB)
cases.reason: TKO loss in the UFC 328 co-main event against Joshua Van; suspended indefinitely until a CT head scan is cleared by a physician, plus a 90-day term with 60 days no contact.
cases.source_quote: Tatsuro Taira: Suspended indefinitely until CT head scan cleared by physician; also suspended 90 days with 60 days no contact for TKO.

cases.jurisdiction: New Jersey State Athletic Control Board (NJSACB)
cases.reason: Left hand injury from the UFC 328 co-main event win over Tatsuro Taira; suspended indefinitely pending orthopedist clearance, plus a 30-day term with 21 days no contact.
cases.source_quote: Joshua Van: Suspended indefinitely until left hand cleared by orthopedist; also suspended 30 days with 21 days no contact.

cases.jurisdiction: New Jersey State Athletic Control Board (NJSACB)
cases.reason: Knockout loss at UFC 328 with a right eye injury; suspended indefinitely until the eye is cleared by an ophthalmologist, plus a 60-day term with 30 days no contact for the knockout.
cases.source_quote: Ozzy Diaz: Suspended indefinitely or until right eye cleared by ophthalmologist; also suspended 60 days with 30 days no contact for knockout.

cases.jurisdiction: New Jersey State Athletic Control Board (NJSACB)
cases.reason: Left hand injury at UFC 328 (Newark, May 9, 2026); suspended indefinitely until the hand and X-rays are cleared by an orthopedist, plus a 30-day term with 15 days no contact.
cases.source_quote: Jim Miller: Suspended indefinitely until left hand and X-rays cleared by orthopedist; also suspended 30 days with 15 days no contact.

cases.reason: Hospitalized after absorbing heavy punishment in his five-round UFC 324 interim-title loss to Justin Gaethje (T-Mobile Arena, Las Vegas); mandatory NSAC medical suspension of at least 30 days under NAC 467.562, and he cannot compete until cleared by a commission-approved doctor.
cases.source_quote: Paddy Pimblett was placed under a mandatory suspension of at least 30 days. The suspension falls under Section 467.562 of the Nevada Administrative Code.

cases.reason: Jaw broken in two places in his sixth-round KO loss to Anthony Joshua on Dec 19, 2025 at the Kaseya Center in Miami; indefinite medical suspension confirmed by the Florida Athletic Commission on Jan 4, 2026, with no return until cleared by a physician.
cases.source_quote: On Sunday, Jan. 4, 2026, the Florida Athletic Commission confirmed Paul's indefinite medical suspension, per MMA Fighting.
